Title: Lactuchelins represent lipopeptide siderophores produced by Pseudomonas lactucae that inhibit Xanthomonas campestris

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron chelation; inhibition of Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris 8004 growth
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated during co-culture with Xcc8004 and Stenotrophomonas rhizophila CFBP 13503 under antagonism-inducing conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated during co-culture with Xcc8004 and Stenotrophomonas rhizophila CFBP 13503 under antagonism-inducing conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: co-culture with Xanthomonas campestris pv. campestris 8004 or certain seed-borne strains; iron availability
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively involved in lactuchelin export/translocation across the cytoplasmic membrane into the periplasm
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-culture induction; biosynthetic gene cluster annotation
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ltcA is part of the induced lactuchelin biosynthetic gene cluster. 2. It encodes an ABC transporter predicted to translocate lactuchelin across the cytoplasmic membrane into the periplasm. 3. The cluster is essential for siderophore-mediated inhibition of Xcc8004.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Transport role was predicted in silico; no direct single-gene knockout validation for ltcA was reported.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could support engineering of complete lactuchelin production/export systems for microbial biocontrol strains; should be stacked with biosynthetic genes.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron acquisition and competitive inhibition of susceptible bacteria through iron sequestration
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Within the induced lactuchelin-associated iron acquisition locus under antagonism-inducing co-culture conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Within the induced lactuchelin-associated iron acquisition locus under antagonism-inducing co-culture conditions
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: co-culture with Xcc8004 or selected seed-borne strains; iron limitation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Probably mediates ferri-lactuchelin uptake into the periplasm and may underlie self-resistance/xenosiderophore utilization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq co-culture induction; genome annotation; comparative genomics
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ltcK is encoded in the lactuchelin locus as a specific TonB-dependent transporter. 2. It is proposed to mediate ferrisiderophore uptake. 3. Presence of LtcK orthologs correlates with potential resistance or piracy of lactuchelins in related Pseudomonas strains.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from annotation; discussed as possible basis for siderophore piracy in P. asgharzadehiana CFBP 13506.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for designing producer strains with self-uptake/self-protection; also relevant to predict resistance of target microbiota.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May facilitate siderophore sharing/piracy by nonproducer strains carrying orthologous receptors.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron chelation and inhibition of Xcc8004 by depriving bioavailable iron
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: amino acid building blocks on peptidyl carrier modules
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes one module of non-ribosomal peptide assembly contributing to lactuchelin peptide backbone formation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for siderophore production, as transposon insertion mutants lost CAS halo formation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: co-culture with Xcc8004 or selected strains; iron depletion enhances metabolite production
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: LtcE; LtcG; LtcH; MbtH-like protein APEGCL_19865
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ltcD belongs to the induced lactuchelin BGC. 2. Transposon disruption abolished siderophore production on CAS medium. 3. It functions as part of the NRPS machinery synthesizing lactuchelins.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Exact module chemistry not individually dissected.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate component for heterologous reconstitution of lactuchelin pathway in biocontrol pseudomonads.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Large NRPS systems may impose metabolic burden.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron chelation and antagonism toward Xcc8004
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: amino acid monomers on NRPS modules
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Carries out module-specific activation/condensation steps during lactuchelin peptide assembly
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for siderophore production based on CAS-negative transposon mutant
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ltcE is part of the lactuchelin NRPS cluster induced during antagonistic interactions. 2. Mutants with insertions in ltcE lost siderophore production phenotype. 3. It is necessary for lactuchelin biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for reconstructing or transferring lactuchelin biosynthetic capacity.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron chelation and growth inhibition of Xcc8004
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: amino acid monomers and acyl/peptidyl intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Participates in non-ribosomal assembly of lactuchelin lipopeptide scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Necessary for siderophore production; insertion mutants lacked CAS orange halo
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ltcG is an NRPS gene in the induced lactuchelin BGC. 2. Two independent transposon insertions in ltcG caused loss of siderophore phenotype. 3. It is part of the core biosynthetic machinery for lactuchelin production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core pathway component for synthetic assembly of lactuchelin production.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron chelation causing growth inhibition of Xcc8004
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: N-hydroxyornithine or ornithine-derived hydroxamate intermediate; acetyl donor not specified
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: acetylated N-hydroxyornithine moiety incorporated into lactuchelin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Acetylates the hydroxylated ornithine-derived residue required for hydroxamate functionality in lactuchelin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: required for formation of iron-chelating functionality of target product
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key required step for lactuchelin production, but rate-limiting not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Deletion abolishes siderophore production and eliminates Xcc8004 growth inhibition; complementation restores both phenotypes partially or fully
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: allelic deletion; chromosomal complementation; CAS assay; Xcc8004 growth inhibition assay; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Within induced BGC under antagonism-inducing co-culture conditions
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Within induced BGC under antagonism-inducing co-culture conditions
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: co-culture with Xcc8004 or selected strains; FeCl3 supplementation abolishes inhibitory phenotype; iron-depleted M9 boosts lactuchelin production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ltcJ is part of the induced lactuchelin biosynthetic cluster and encodes a predicted N-hydroxyornithine acetyltransferase. 2. RB-TnSeq insertion and clean deletion of ltcJ abolish siderophore production on CAS medium. 3. ltcJ deletion removes the ability of P. lactucae CFBP 13502 supernatant to inhibit Xcc8004, and complementation restores the phenotype. 4. Metabolomics confirmed lactuchelins are absent in the ltcJ mutant and restored at lower levels in the complemented strain.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In planta seed-to-seedling transmission assay showed no detectable contribution of lactuchelin/ltcJ under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong target for engineering microbial biocontrol agents that suppress phytopathogens by iron sequestration; should be deployed with full cluster and tested under relevant iron conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May inhibit non-target susceptible bacteria and impose metabolic cost; no benefit detected in seedling transmission assay under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Plpp promoter in cis-complementation construct pUC18T-miniTn7-PlppGFP-KmR-ltcJ

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates aspartate to generate β-hydroxyaspartate incorporated into lactuchelin
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies modified building block for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to provide β-hydroxyaspartate modifications present in lactuchelin structure
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BGC annotation; structural characterization of product
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NRPS assembly proteins in lactuchelin cluster
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ltcC is predicted to encode an aspartate β-hydroxylase in the lactuchelin cluster. 2. The final product contains two β-hydroxyaspartate residues consistent with this assignment. 3. It likely contributes precursor tailoring required for iron-chelating lactuchelin structure.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Needed for faithful reconstruction of active lactuchelin scaffold.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates ornithine to generate hydroxamate precursor for lactuchelin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies modified precursor essential for target product iron-binding group
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Predicted to generate N-hydroxyornithine precursor required for lactuchelin hydroxamate chemistry
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: BGC annotation; structural characterization of product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ltcI_1 is predicted to encode L-ornithine N(5)-monooxygenase in the lactuchelin BGC. 2. This activity explains the N-hydroxyornithine-derived hydroxamate moiety of lactuchelins. 3. It likely works together with LtcJ to form the iron-binding residue.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A second homolog, ltcI_2 (APEGCL_18425), lies outside the BGC and shares 72% nucleotide identity with ltcI_1.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important pathway component for restoring full siderophore iron-binding chemistry in engineered strains.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: iron chelation and antagonism toward Xcc8004
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates lactuchelin biosynthetic gene expression, inferred from loss-of-function mutant phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: likely low iron; possibly strain-specific intermicrobial signals during co-culture
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential positive regulator candidate for lactuchelin production because pvdS transposon mutation abolished siderophore phenotype
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Mutation abolishes siderophore production, indicating PvdS is required for lactuchelin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RB-TnSeq/CAS plate mutant screening; transcriptomic inference; discussion-based mechanistic interpretation
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Associated with iron-responsive induction context; exact expression not separately reported
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Associated with iron-responsive induction context; exact expression not separately reported
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: co-culture with Xcc8004 or selected strains; probable iron depletion or signaling molecules
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. pvdS was identified in CAS-negative transposon mutants defective in siderophore production. 2. The paper proposes that PvdS activates lactuchelin biosynthetic genes even though the strain lacks the pyoverdine BGC. 3. PvdS likely links iron-responsive or interaction-responsive signaling to lactuchelin production.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Regulatory role for lactuchelin genes is inferred, not directly shown by promoter-binding assays.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever to inducibly activate lactuchelin production in biocontrol strains; requires caution because activation may depend on iron context and unknown interspecies signals.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could broadly alter iron acquisition programs and cellular physiology.
